title: Unable to use VM's proxy for container build step for installing dependencies
body:
### Issue Description

Loopback address of the container is used instead of the host vm's causing installation failures. However, pulling images is not a problem if the proxy is exported into the terminal session.  

Using 10.0.2.2 or host.containers.internal did not work either. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. start a debian 12 vm
2. export your proxy into a terminal session 
3. podman pull an official image
4. unset your proxy
5. follow official proxy configuration instructions

in rootless: `$HOME/.config/containers/containers.conf`

```
[engine]

env = ["http_proxy=<127.0.0.1:port>", "https_proxy=<your.proxy.tld:port>"]
]
```
also
``` 
[engine]
env = ["http_proxy=host.containers.internal:port>", "https_proxy=host.containers.internal:port>"]
```
additionally:
```
[containers]
env = [
'https_proxy=<host.containers.internal:port>',
```
6. podman build -t etc


OR 
1. podman system reset
2. export your proxy into a terminal session 
3. cd into project directory 
4. container file with image pull then dependency installation step
5. podman build -t etc
6. image pulls
7. build fails at dependency installation

I am only labeling this as a bug because I am able to pull images successfully and the first part on the build step works by exporting the proxy in a terminal session only. However, the above settings will not work if the proxy is unset in the terminal and the user follows the official configuration instructions. Additionally the VM's proxy cannot be used for the container either with configuration steps or exporting it in a terminal. 

Steps after pulling an image in build will not work with or without the proxy exported into the terminal or "passed" to the container using container env settings.







### Describe the results you received

failure to connect to the the network. Retries several times then fails. Build runs successfully if dependency installation steps in the container are left out. 

### Describe the results you expected

To be able to use the proxy used by the VM running podman build steps.

It is my understanding that the loopback address of the container will be used once the image is built during the build step and the configuration step set as host.containers.internal would mitigate this problem. Additionally the engine does not respect the configuration settings and will only work if exported into the terminal. Even such, the build will fail if their are instructions to install dependencies in the container (e.g., RUN pip install or RUN bun install).   

### podman info output

```yaml
Debian 12 
4.3.1 Podman
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Running podman inside a Debian 12 VM.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
If you have proxy environment variables exported in your terminal session and your proxy server is on localhost, then try the following:

```toml
[containers]
http_proxy = false
env = [
  "http_proxy=http://host.containers.internal:<port>",
  "https_proxy=http://host.containers.internal:<port>",
]
```

Also, try adding `RUN env` in your `Containerfile` to see what value the environment variables are set to.

Hello thank you. 

Unfortunately adding `http_proxy = false` did not rectify the issue.

Using these settings made it so I no longer needed to export the proxy in the terminal session to pull the images successfully.

``` 
[engine]
env = [
  "https_proxy=http://host.containers.internal:<port>",
]
[container]
http_proxy = false
env = [
  "http_proxy=http://host.containers.internal:<port>",
  "https_proxy=http://host.containers.internal:<port>",
]
```

However, the second step which is installing dependencies inside the container still fails. 


getting the following at RUN env step 
```
HOME=/root
https_proxy=http://127.0.0.1:portnumber
http_proxy=http://127.0.0.1:portnumber/
Path=/usr/local/sbin:/etc.,
PWD=/app
```
then hangs at install step 
verbose log messages refer to connection issues
at the install step


Here are all the settings combinations I have tried
```
[container]
http_proxy = false
env = [
  "http_proxy=http://host.containers.internal:<port>",
  "https_proxy=http://host.containers.internal:<port>",
]
```
OR
```
[engine]
env = ["https_proxy=http://host.containers.internal:<port>"]

[container]
http_proxy = false
env = [
  "http_proxy=http://host.containers.internal:<port>",
  "https_proxy=http://host.containers.internal:<port>",
]
```
OR
```
[engine]
env = ["https_proxy=http://127.0.0.1:<port>"]

[container]
http_proxy = false
env = [
  "http_proxy=http://host.containers.internal:<port>",
  "https_proxy=http://host.containers.internal:<port>",
```
All fail in the same way. 


Is there any root system files that are modified or altered (in rootless mode) while these network settings instantiate? If so that may answer the issue on my end. Otherwise, I am unsure what is causing this bug. Debug logs have it stall out after closing stdin. verbose logs on the install still indicate network connectivity issues.

> getting the following at RUN env step
> 
> ```
> HOME=/root
> https_proxy=http://127.0.0.1:portnumber
> http_proxy=http://127.0.0.1:portnumber/
> Path=/usr/local/sbin:/etc.,
> PWD=/app
> ```

Please check where this is coming from, and try to unset it. If your intention is to use proxy server on your host instead of in the container, the `http[s]_proxy` host in the container should be set to `host.containers.internal`, not `localhost`.

hello, i can try tackling this issue
/assign
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
